Description: Iraq invades Kuwait leading to immediate global energy security anxieties and supply premium hikes.
Triggers massive contraction in global aviation fuel consumption followed by premium pricing adjustments.
Catalyzes global financial crisis; widespread global industrial demand destruction.
Widespread regional civil unrest halts sweet crude export flows across North Africa.
OPEC refuses to cut production targets despite expanding US shale output crashing global pricing regimes.
US pulls out of JCPOA nuclear framework reimposing aggressive sanctions targeting Iranian crude exports.
Drone strikes disable Saudi Aramco processing nodes knocking offline 5% of active global supply.
Disagreements on production caps spark an aggressive volume war between Russia and Saudi Arabia.
Enforced global structural lockdowns induce unprecedented historic drops in industrial fuel demand.
Extreme domestic infrastructure storage shortages drop WTI below zero disrupting global spot benchmarks.
Outbreak of active European warfare and widespread Western economic sanctions send Brent soaring near 140 USD.
OPEC+ imposes aggressive production rollbacks to artificially defend falling baseline target lines.
